mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction activity combined with stable short-term interest rates signals robust aggregate demand without inflationary pressure, boosting equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint momentum of HOUST and FEDFUNDS shows no predictive power for 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02) across two full market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% annualized rate indicates strong manufacturing capacity utilization, which historically precedes positive equity returns over the next week as corporate margins expand.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% over a 3-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly reflects expanding corporate revenue streams, generating positive forward equity returns over the subsequent month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes across a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0% year-over-year, inflation expectations force the Federal Reserve to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with rising consumer credit balances indicates sustainable household spending power, which drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict 60-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over two consecutive market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts during a period of high interest rates signals a demand shock that reduces corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when HOUST declines for three consecutive months across a 5-year walk-forward test.
